paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments
summary: Conjecture 11 from arXiv:2410.23566 asserts that the k-extension operation preserves linear unavoidability for any family of digraphs. The source paper itself proves a special case (Corollary 25): the family of k-extensions of oriented forests is linearly unavoidable. The general conjecture is noted to follow from the stronger Conjecture 9 (that adding a single vertex multiplies the unavoidability constant by at most a constant). No follow-up paper resolving or making further progress on the general conjecture was found in a wide web search through May 2026.
search_queries: linearly unavoidable k-extensions digraphs tournaments Aboulker Havet 2410.23566
linearly unavoidable family digraphs k-extension operation graph theory arxiv 2025
"linearly unavoidable" "k-extension" tournaments digraphs Aboulker Havet Picasarri-Arrieta 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up work found. The conjecture is recent (October 2024) and the paper proves it for the special case of oriented trees/forests (Corollary 25). The general statement would follow from Conjecture 9 of the same paper. A search of Picasarri-Arrieta's publication page (post-Oct 2024 preprints) revealed no directly relevant follow-up.
